mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding corporate revenue and household income; positive payroll growth directly drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months of positive PAYEMS growth do not exceed those following negative growth (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% structurally elevates the equity discount rate and tightens financial conditions; sustained restrictive policy produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC > 0 over any 2-year window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The multiplicative interaction between the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) encodes stagflationary risk; elevated levels of both series jointly compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A linear model with FEDFUNDS and UNRATE interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) for 20-day returns, or the interaction-driven portfolio underperforms an additive linear model in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal imminent earnings compression from falling capacity utilization; these declines precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55% of late-cycle instances, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,550 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment and downstream construction demand; this threshold triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations reset.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1,550, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50% over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) demonstrates operating leverage and pricing power in supply-constrained environments; this growth drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as margin expansion expectations rise.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 2-year rolling window, or returns following positive growth fail to beat cash (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction term between industrial production (INDPRO) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) captures non-linear business cycle risk; this interaction negatively predicts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because simultaneous weakness compounds earnings uncertainty.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE delivers no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) for 20-day returns over a 3-year window, or the interaction-driven portfolio underperforms a linear two-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1), reported with a 6-month lag, signals persistent demand underperformance that the equity market slowly incorporates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following negative GDPC1 growth are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC exceeds 0 over any 3-year rolling window.
